title: narcissus hispanicus subsp. longispathus
intro: Narcissus hispanicus subsp. longispathus, also known as the Spanish bluebell or bluebells, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Narcissus. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cognitive function, and possessing antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Narcissus hispanicus subsp. longispathus can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, but its use should be guided by a healthcare professional due to potential toxicity and interactions with medications.
